user_query: I’m trying to improve on-page SEO for my tiny history blog so more people can find my research while I practice for a future historical novel. Please audit all HTML pages in input/site/ using the target keywords in input/keywords.csv, then produce a structured report and a set of actionable notes I can discuss with a friend who helps me with the site. Requirements: 1) Inputs and mapping - Read all .html files under input/site/. - Read input/keywords.csv, which maps a page path (e.g., /roman-legions.html) to a target_keyword and intent. Each CSV path corresponds to the local file at input/site{path}. 2) Extract for each page (case-insensitive checks where relevant) - path: the CSV path that matches the file (e.g., /index.html). - title: text inside . - title_length: character length of the title string. - meta_description: content of <meta name='description'> if present; otherwise empty. - meta_description_length: character length of meta_description (0 if missing or empty). - h1: text inside the first <h1>. - has_canonical: true if a <link rel='canonical'> exists; false otherwise. - internal_link_count: number of <a> tags whose href starts with '/', counted anywhere in the document. - image_count: number of <img> tags. - images_missing_alt: number of <img> tags without an alt attribute or with an empty alt. - word_count_main: total word count of the visible text inside <main> (ignore tags, scripts, and styles; split on whitespace for words). - target_keyword: from keywords.csv for this path. - keyword_in_title: true if target_keyword appears as a substring in title. - keyword_in_h1: true if target_keyword appears as a substring in h1. - keyword_in_meta_description: true if target_keyword appears as a substring in meta_description. - keyword_in_first_100_words: true if target_keyword appears as a substring within the first 100 words of the <main> text (after stripping HTML and normalizing case). 3) Outputs (write all files) - output/audit.csv: one row per page with the fields listed above, sorted by path ascending. - output/audit.json: the same records as an array of objects with identical field names and values. - output/seo_action_items.md: meeting-style notes that include: - A concise summary of key findings (e.g., missing meta descriptions, images without alt, long titles, keyword placement issues), referencing affected pages. - A prioritized Top 5 fixes with rationales that tie directly to items observable in output/audit.csv (e.g., add missing meta description for /medieval-village.html, add alt text, add canonical, adjust title length, include target keyword early in <main>). - Page-specific checklists (at least 3 bullet items per page) with concrete actions aligned to the audit (e.g., “Add 1–2 internal links to related page X”, “Place target keyword in first paragraph”, “Write a 150–160 character meta description,” etc.). 4) Notes - Treat all string comparisons for keyword presence as case-insensitive and based on simple substring containment after trimming and normalizing whitespace. - Do not use the internet; analyze only the provided files. Inputs are in input/, and outputs must be written exactly to output/audit.csv, output/audit.json, and output/seo_action_items.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
